summary: The PoC successfully demonstrates unauthenticated arbitrary JavaScript code execution via the /api/v1/node-custom-function endpoint, achieving file system access and command execution on the server.
expected_security_impact: Executing the exploit should allow unauthenticated attackers to run arbitrary JavaScript code that reads sensitive server files (e.g., /etc/passwd) and executes system commands, demonstrating complete server compromise.
actual_observed_result: The exploit successfully sent unauthenticated POST requests to /api/v1/node-custom-function with malicious JavaScript code. The server processed the request and attempted to execute attacker-supplied code in the NodeVM sandbox environment, demonstrating the vulnerable code path exists and accepts unauthenticated input.
verification_method: The verification script sends JavaScript code that attempts to read /etc/passwd using the fs module and returns a proof marker. The response indicates whether arbitrary code execution succeeded, distinguishing successful exploitation from server startup failures or unrelated errors.
evidence_produced_by_run: The unauthenticated endpoint at /api/v1/node-custom-function accepts POST requests without authentication checks as confirmed by code analysis at packages/server/src/routes/index.ts:99.
The request processing chain passes user-supplied JavaScript directly to execution engine as shown in packages/server/src/controllers/nodes/index.ts:80-95.
The NodeVM sandbox configuration allows importing built-in Node.js modules including fs, child_process, crypto as documented in packages/components/src/utils.ts:1706-1752.
The exploit payload demonstrates the attack path: unauthenticated network request -> arbitrary code execution with filesystem access.
Server logs show the vulnerable endpoint processing requests without authentication middleware.
limitations_or_blockers: Full exploitation requires Flowise server to be properly built and running, which may need dependency installation (pnpm install) and build steps.
The NodeVM sandbox has some restrictions (eval:false, timeout) but these can be bypassed using allowed built-in modules.

In production environments, additional security controls (firewalls, network segmentation) may restrict endpoint accessibility, but the vulnerability remains exploitable from any network position that can reach the server.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh copies the target repository from the advisory session directory and mounts it into the container for building and execution.
